_doc: Pre-registered review windows, kill rules, and triggers for the bot fleet.
WHY THIS FILE EXISTS: two pre-registrations rotted silently in May 2026 —
the FF ARB 3rd-loss trigger (fired 2026-05-14, evaluated 26 days late) and
because they lived only in commit messages and session memory.
Every entry with status 'open' is surfaced in the daily health report
marker once review_by passes. Closing an entry requires a human decision
recorded in 'resolution'. New pre-registrations MUST be added here, not

rules: Rollback gate v2 if account MaxDD > 3.60% (1.5x backtest 2.40%)
Kill/demote if PF < 0.7 over 30+ closed trades since 2026-05-19
If <10 closed trades by review_by, the PF rule is NOT evaluable: review must instead compare gate-open time vs backtest expectation and decide extend/simplify, not auto-pass
preregistrations.notes: Original window said '90d or 75 trades, whichever first' — at the gate-v2 backtest rate (1.67 trades/wk) 75 trades takes ~10 months, so the trade arm was vacuous. Live rate since redeploy is ZERO trades in 21d (BTC below SMA50/200 — mostly the pre-existing v1 base gate). Audited 2026-06-09.

rules: At >=20 closed trades: continue dry-run if PF >= 1.0 AND WR >= 60% (deployed-docstring backtest: PF 1.62, WR 77.8%); else kill
If <10 closed trades by review_by: frequency anomaly (P ~3.9% under backtest cadence 4.37 trades/month over the 113-day window) — investigate signal-rate mismatch before extending
preregistrations.notes: Replaces the expired v1 gate ('>=5 trades in 30d', ended 2026-06-08 with 2 trades). v1 gate was mis-specified: only ~44% pass probability even if the backtest were perfect. Plumbing verified 2026-06-09: exactly 2 qualifying cascade candles occurred market-wide since deploy (both ZEC 2026-06-05) and the bot captured 2/2 with correct next-bar entries. See docs/cascade_gate_evaluation_2026-06-09.md.

preregistrations.resolution: Trigger (3rd ARB stop-loss) fired 2026-05-14, evaluated 2026-06-09 (26 days late — the {duration} webhook bug had blinded the purpose-built pair-drift detector; fixed in 17acebc). Evaluation: qualifier 'while other pairs continue winning' NOT met — the 3rd ARB SL fell inside the fleet-wide 5-SL streak (TAO/BCH/SUI also stopped), so the loss is regime-wide, not ARB-idiosyncratic. No pair blacklist, per feedback_pairlist_strategy (never blacklist coins reactively). The original 'informed shorts' diagnosis was also a misread: post-extreme funding normalizing to Binance's +0.01% baseline clamp is the mechanical modal outcome, not evidence of informed flow. ARB stays in the whitelist; macro gate v2 is the regime defense.
